title: papaver armeniacum subsp. microstigmum
intro: Papaver armeniacum subsp. microstigmum, also known as Armenian poppy or Armenian poppy subsp. microstigmum, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) native to the mountainous regions of Turkey and Armenia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is also known to have antioxidant, antimicrobial, and wound-healing properties. The health benefits of Papaver armeniacum subsp. microstigmum include the potential to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Papaver armeniacum subsp. microstigmum include tinctures, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
